summary: Typosquat of `dotenv` pulling in third-party `environment-gate` invoked from `config()`
details: Package is published as `dotenv-express` but its README and branding masquerade as the legitimate `dotenv` package (`# dotenv [NPM version]...`, `npm install dotenv`). It replicates the upstream `dotenv` API surface, then adds `const gate = require('environment-gate')` at the top of `lib/main.js` and invokes `gate.gate()` as the first statement inside the exported `config()` function — behavior absent from `motdotla/dotenv`. Installers who mistype `dotenv` as `dotenv-express` and call the documented `.config()` entry point will silently execute code from the separately-published `environment-gate` dependency. The lure package is a thin harness whose only divergence from upstream is to route control flow into a third-party module on the first call to the most common dotenv API.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
